text: Each motor carrier shall, at least once every twelve months, require each bus driver it employs to prepare and furnish it with a list of all violations of motor vehicle traffic laws and ordinances (other than violations involving only parking) of which the driver has been convicted or on account of which he has forfeited bond or collateral and all accidents of a nature or type set forth in § 509-A (Definitions) in which the driver was involved during the preceding twelve months whether such violations or accidents occurred in this state or elsewhere. markdown:
# Vehicle & Traffic Law
## Title 5
#### Article 19
##### § 509-F — Record of violations

Each motor carrier shall, at least once every twelve months, require each bus driver it employs to prepare and furnish it with a list of all violations of motor vehicle traffic laws and ordinances (other than violations involving only parking) of which the driver has been convicted or on account of which he has forfeited bond or collateral and all accidents of a nature or type set forth in § 509-A (Definitions) in which the driver was involved during the preceding twelve months whether such violations or accidents occurred in this state or elsewhere. Such information shall be recorded in the employer’s record.

*Last modified: Sep. 22, 2014*

Such information shall be recorded in the employer’s record.
